steps.title: Fastest path: track the scale first
steps.content: Track the relevant dimensions or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Convert cubic meters to cubic centimeters
Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• A: 0.08 - far too small; this would require almost no unit conversion at all. Eliminate A.
  • B: 6.81 - off by a factor of about 100; likely from converting m to cm without cubing. Eliminate B.
  • C: 681.37 - close but arises from using the quart conversion factor in the wrong direction. Eliminate C.
  • D: 760.82 - matches $720{,}000 \div 946.35 \approx 760.8$. D is correct.
